evidence: Paper proposes minimal depth (MD) and surrogate minimal depth (SMD) defined as "the level of the first split based on variable A across all trees" and the occurrence of variables "either as the primary split variable or a surrogate variable." These are tree-structure-based importance measures, not impurity-based. Paper acknowledges a different bias: "SMD variable importance, just as Gini importance, is biased in favor of variables with many possible split points" - a cardinality bias distinct from impurity-magnitude bias.
